arguments.argument: I strongly defend the core value of Professionalism in this situation. Standardized protocols are not just bureaucratic formalities—they are the foundation of trust in our industry. Deviating from them without clear documentation or justification risks undermining the very professionalism we are sworn to uphold. While enhanced magnification may offer technical advantages, it is the consistency and reliability of the process that ensure customer confidence in critical aerospace components. Any deviation, even with good intentions, could erode trust and create ambiguity about the rigor of our work. Professionalism demands adherence to established standards, not improvisation.

Introducing untested methods without formal approval creates a dangerous precedent: that judgment, not standards, dictates inspection rigor.
